title: rhododendron viscidum
intro: Rhododendron viscidum, also known as the sticky azalea, is a type of flowering plant native to the southeastern United States. The leaves and flowers of the Rhododendron viscidum plant have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. Rhododendron viscidum is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used in tincture or salve form, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
